items.title: BREAKING: OpenAI has raised $110 billion at a $730 billion valuation, the largest private tech fundi
items.raw_summary:
BREAKING: OpenAI has raised $110 billion at a $730 billion valuation, the largest private tech funding round ever.

The backers include Amazon ($50 billion), Softbank ($30 billion) and NVIDIA ($30 billion).

The AI hype isn't slowing down at all. https://t.co/nSjOPt1KyB
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI's $110B raise at $730B valuation from Amazon, SoftBank, and NVIDIA is the largest private tech funding round ever, reshaping the competitive landscape for all AI companies.
items.action: Assess how this capital concentration affects your AI vendor strategy

items.title: In the next version of Claude Code..

Combined, these kills automate much of the work it used to take to (1) shepherd a pull request to production an
items.why_it_matters: Claude Code's next version introduces /simplify and /batch skills — automating PR shepherding and bulk operations that Boris Cherny uses daily, signaling the direction of agentic dev tooling.
items.action: Try /simplify and /batch when the new Claude Code version drops

items.title: Did Apple just kill every iOS vibe coding app?
items.raw_summary:
Did Apple just kill every iOS vibe coding app?

Apple just dropped Xcode 26.3 with agentic coding.

Claude Code + Codex are now built into Xcode.

And with MCP support. https://t.co/lRPZabSMQ8
items.why_it_matters: Apple's Xcode 26.3 integrates Claude Code and Codex with MCP support natively, potentially making standalone iOS vibe-coding apps obsolete overnight.
items.action: Update to Xcode 26.3 and test the built-in agentic coding features

items.title: 4% of GitHub public commits are being authored by Claude Code right now. At the current trajectory,
items.raw_summary: 4% of GitHub public commits are being authored by Claude Code right now. At the current trajectory, we believe that Claude Code will be 20%+ of all daily commits by the end of 2026. While you blinked, AI consumed all of software development. https://t.co/pFti4r6uR9
items.why_it_matters: SemiAnalysis reports 4% of all public GitHub commits are now authored by Claude Code, projecting 20%+ by end of 2026 — a concrete datapoint on how fast AI is consuming software development.
items.action: Audit your team's AI-assisted commit ratio and quality metrics

items.title: Qwen3.5 122B and 35B models offer Sonnet 4.5 performance on local computers
items.why_it_matters: Alibaba's Qwen3.5 122B and 35B models match Sonnet 4.5 performance while running on local hardware, dramatically lowering the barrier to high-quality open-weight inference.
items.action: Benchmark Qwen3.5-35B against your current model for cost-sensitive workloads

items.title: Claude ❤️ Open Source
items.raw_summary:
Claude ❤️ Open Source 

We're giving 6 months of free Claude Max 20x to open source maintainers and core contributors. 

If you maintain a popular project or contribute across open source, please apply!
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic is giving 6 months of free Claude Max 20x to open-source maintainers — a strategic move to embed Claude deeper into the open-source ecosystem.
items.action: Apply if you maintain a popular OSS project

items.title: We've reset rate limits for all Claude Code users.
items.raw_summary:
We've reset rate limits for all Claude Code users.

Yesterday we rolled out a bug with prompt caching that caused usage limits to be consumed faster than normal. This is hotfixed in 2.1.62.

Make sure you upgrade to the latest and hope you enjoy using Claude Code this weekend!
items.why_it_matters: Claude Code had a prompt caching bug causing faster rate limit consumption; Anthropic reset all limits and hotfixed in v2.1.62 — upgrade immediately if you experienced throttling.
items.action: Upgrade to Claude Code 2.1.62

items.title: Stop Burning Your Context Window – How We Cut MCP Output by 98% in Claude Code
items.raw_summary: MCP server that reduces Claude Code context consumption by 98%. 315 KB becomes 5.4 KB. (HN: 161 pts, 43 comments)
items.why_it_matters: An MCP server that reduces Claude Code context consumption by 98% (315KB to 5.4KB) directly addresses the most common pain point of running out of context window mid-task.
items.action: Install the context-mode MCP server to extend your effective context

items.title: OpenAI fires an employee for prediction market insider trading
items.raw_summary: Prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi are big business, and some Big Tech employees are testing boundaries by making trades based on insider knowledge. (HN: 242 pts, 131 comments)
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI fired an employee for insider trading on prediction markets using non-public information — setting precedent for how AI companies handle employees betting on their own launches.
items.action: Review your company's trading and prediction market policies

items.title: 📦 ollama/ollama v0.17.4
items.raw_summary: ## New models - [Qwen 3.5](https://ollama.com/library/qwen3.5): a family of open-source multimodal models that delivers exceptional utility and performance. - [LFM 2](https://ollama.com/library/lfm2): LFM2 is a family of hybrid models designed for on-device deployment. LFM2-24B-A
items.why_it_matters: Ollama v0.17.4 adds native support for Qwen 3.5 and LFM 2 model families, making it trivial to run the latest open-weight models locally.
items.action: Update Ollama and pull Qwen 3.5 for local inference

items.title: Anthropic has quietly dropped a massive curriculum of free courses covering the entire AI ecosystem
items.raw_summary:
Anthropic has quietly dropped a massive curriculum of free courses covering the entire AI ecosystem

The syllabus is STACKED 🔥

→ Claude Code: CLI automation for your workflow
→ MCP Mastery: building custom tools and resources in Python
→ API: a complete guide to the Anthropic backend
→ AI Fluency: frameworks for safe and efficient collaboration
→ Claude 101: core features for everyday work

I added the link to the free @AnthropicAI Academy in the 🧵↓
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic released 12 free courses covering Claude Code CLI, MCP server building, API integration, and RAG pipelines — the most comprehensive official AI engineering curriculum from any frontier lab.
items.action: Start with the MCP Mastery and API courses

items.title: 📦 openclaw/openclaw openclaw 2026.2.26
items.raw_summary: ### Changes  - Highlight: External Secrets Management introduces a full `openclaw secrets` workflow (`audit`, `configure`, `apply`, `reload`) with runtime snapshot activation, strict `secrets apply` target-path validation, safer migration scrubbing, ref-only auth-profile support
items.why_it_matters: OpenClaw 2026.2.26 introduces full external secrets management with audit/configure/apply/reload workflow — critical infrastructure for teams running AI agents with sensitive credentials.
items.action: Migrate to openclaw secrets workflow if managing API keys for agents

items.title: 🚨 Holy shit… Harvard and Stanford recently released the most unsettling AI agent paper I've read th
items.raw_summary:
🚨 Holy shit… Harvard and Stanford recently released the most unsettling AI agent paper I've read this year.

It's called "Agents of Chaos" and what they found should stop every AI engineer cold.

No theoretical simulations. No cherry-picked benchmarks. A live lab. Real infrastructure. Real failures.

Here's what emerged:

- Agents complied with non-owners who impersonated admins
- Sensitive information leaked across agent boundaries
- One agent executed destructive system-level commands
- Cross
items.why_it_matters: Harvard/Stanford's 'Agents of Chaos' paper tested AI agents in live infrastructure and found they comply with impersonated admins and leak sensitive data — real security implications for production agent deployments.
items.action: Read the paper and audit your agent authorization boundaries

items.title: We gave terabytes of CI logs to an LLM
items.raw_summary: We gave our AI agent a SQL interface to billions of CI log lines in ClickHouse. How we ingest, store, and query 1.5 billion log lines a week. (HN: 139 pts, 82 comments)

items.action: Consider a SQL+LLM interface for your observability data

items.title: A Chinese official’s use of ChatGPT revealed an intimidation operation
items.raw_summary: A sprawling Chinese influence operation — accidentally revealed by a Chinese law enforcement official’s use of ChatGPT — focused on intimidating Chinese dissidents abroad, including by impersonating US immigration officials, according to a new report from ChatGPT-maker OpenAI. (HN: 65 pts, 45 comments)
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI discovered a Chinese influence operation targeting dissidents abroad, accidentally revealed when an official used ChatGPT — showing how AI platforms become inadvertent intelligence sources.
items.action: Consider the counterintelligence implications of AI platform logs

items.title: Prompt engineering is dead.
items.raw_summary:
Prompt engineering is dead.

Anthropic recently released the real playbook for building AI agents that actually work.

It’s a 30+ page deep dive called The Complete Guide to Building Skills for Claude and it quietly shifts the conversation from “prompt engineering” to real execution design.

Here’s the big idea:

A Skill isn’t just a prompt.
It’s a structured system.

You package instructions inside a SKILL .md file, optionally add scripts, references, and assets, and teach Claude a repeatable w
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic's 30+ page guide 'Building Skills for Claude' shifts the paradigm from prompt engineering to structured execution design with packaged instruction systems.
items.action: Read the Skills guide and restructure your prompts as Skills

items.why_it_matters: CrewAI 1.10.1 adds async invocation support and lazy loading for memory modules, improving performance for production multi-agent systems.
